description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the node:alpine Docker image with three stages: install, build, and publish. In the install stage add an install job that pulls and pushes a cache keyed by ${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG} containing node_modules/, and runs `yarn install`. In the build stage add a build job that runs on all branches except tags matching /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+$/ , /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+-alpha\.\d+$/ and /^\d+\.\d+\.\d+-beta\.\d+$/, executing `yarn install`, `yarn lint`, `yarn test`, and `yarn run build`. Also in the build stage add a build:release job that runs only on those version tags, changes directory to functions, runs `yarn install` and `yarn run build`, and stores the resulting functions/dist directory as an artifact that expires in one week. Finally, in the publish stage add a deploy job that depends on build:release, runs only on the same version tags, copies .npmrc.sample to .npmrc, runs `yarn copy-package-json` and `yarn publish dist --new-version $CI_COMMIT_TAG`, and publishes the package to the npm registry under the npm_publish environment.
